LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT BECAUSE ON APRIL 15, 2019, PLAINTIFF FILED A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE FILING OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT COMPLIED WITH CODE CIV. PROC. 472(A) ("A PARTY MAY AMEND ITS PLEADING ONCE WITHOUT LEAVE OF THE COURT AT ANY TIME BEFORE THE ANSWER, DEMURRER, OR MOTION TO STRIKE IS FILED, OR AFTER A DEMURRER OR MOTION TO STRIKE IS FILED BUT BEFORE THE DEMURRER OR MOTION TO STRIKE IS HEARD IF THE AMENDED PLEADING IS FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN THE DATE FOR FILING AN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER OR MOTION TO STRIKE"). IN THIS CASE, WHILE PLAINTIFF ORIGINALLY FILED AN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER, HE SUBSEQUENTLY FILED AND SERVED A TIMELY AMENDED COMPLAINT. PURSUANT TO CODE CIV. PROC. 1005(B), PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS DUE ON APRIL 17, 2019. PLAINTIFF SERVED THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ON APRIL 9, 2019 AND FILED IT ON APRIL 15, 2019. DEFENDANT IS FREE TO DEMUR TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IF APPROPRIATE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)
ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER AND DISMISSING PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAC DOES NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS BECAUSE IT DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT THE CITY IS INDEBTED TO PLAINTIFF FOR A SPECIFIC SUM OR THAT PLAINTIFF PROVIDED GOODS OR SERVICES TO THE CITY FOR WHICH HE WAS UNCOMPENSATED. (SEE FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE V. ZERIN (1997) 53 CAL.APP.4TH 445, 460 [ESSENTIAL ALLEGATIONS OF A COMMON COUNT ARE THE STATEMENT OF INDEBTEDNESS IN A CERTAIN, THE CONSIDERATION, I.E., GOODS SOLD, WORK DONE, ETC., AND NONPAYMENT].) HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO STATE A VIABLE CAUSE OF ACTION ARISING OUT OF THE FACTS ALLEGED, THE THRUST OF WHICH IS THAT THE CITY DID NOT HIRE HIM AS A TRUCK DRIVER IN VIOLATION OF ITS PUBLISHED POLICIES. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Defendants Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Dec-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rose Darline, Esq., City Attorney, CCSF, 415-554-3959, appearing for Defendant CCSF;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Defendants Discovery Requests

Motion to compel GRANTED. Because responses were not timely served, all objections were waived. CCP 2030.290, 2031.300. Plaintiff's late responses to the requests for production (attached to the reply) are rejected because the time for serving objections had expired. Complete responses to interrogatories and requests for production to be received by Defendant no later than January 3, 2020.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rose Darling, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3959) for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO Plaintiff's 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. The FAC does not state a cause of action for common counts because it does not allege that the City is indebted to Plaintiff for a specific sum or that Plaintiff provided goods or services to the City for which he was uncompensated. (See Farmers Insurance Exchange v. Zerin (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 445, 460 [essential allegations of a common count are the statement of indebtedness in a certain, the consideration, i.e., goods sold, work done, etc., and nonpayment].) However, Plaintiff may be able to state a viable cause of action arising out of the facts alleged, the thrust of which is that the City did not hire him as a truck driver in violation of its published policies. Plaintiff is granted 20 days leave to amend.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to complaint is off calendar as moot because on April 15, 2019, Plaintiff filed a first amended complaint. The filing of the amended complaint complied with Code Civ. Proc. 472(a) ("A party may amend its pleading once without leave of the court at any time before the answer, demurrer, or motion to strike is filed, or after a demurrer or motion to strike is filed but before the demurrer or motion to strike is heard if the amended pleading is filed and served no later than the date for filing an opposition to the demurrer or motion to strike"). In this case, while Plaintiff originally filed an opposition to the demurrer, he subsequently filed and served a timely amended complaint. Pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. 1005(b), Plaintiff's opposition was due on April 17, 2019. Plaintiff served the amended complaint on April 9, 2019 and filed it on April 15, 2019. Defendant is free to demur to the amended complaint if appropriate.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
